description: An object conveying information or instruction; to make known through gesture or mark. 'Siroa' names what speaks without voice—the visible communication.
sound_symbolism: The word shows with clarity. 'Si' sees with visible form, sign appearing. 'Ro' reveals with opening recognition. Then 'a' opens to full meaning, message received.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo siroa' (the sign). As verb: 'mi siroa' (I sign/indicate). More communicative than 'siru' (marker)—conveys message, not just location.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Signs point to meaning—the sign is not the thing itself but indication toward understanding.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Signs enable communication—shared symbols allowing understanding across languages.
